title: Hiyori and Yasuie Reveal Wano’s Pain
description: On pages 14–17, this part focuses on Unknown person #13, Hyogoro, Caribou, Unknown person #14, Unknown person #15, Orochi as Hiyori’s identity and Yasuie’s sacrifice pull the Wano rebellion into a deeper emotional crisis.
scene: Zoro protects Toko and meets Hiyori, while Yasuie prepares a sacrifice meant to rescue the rebellion’s plan.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Also would never have believed

dialogue.speaker: Hyogoro
dialogue.text: that you were HYogoro himself...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: I CAN'T BELIEVE IT!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So are you saying that all of you came from twenty years ago?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Indeed! Thanks to Lady Toki's magical powers...

dialogue.speaker: Caribou
dialogue.text: I don't believe you, caribou!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Come now, don't say such a thing, straw hat!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: C'mon, Raiza! I thought that was the key to my handcuffs, but it turns out it's for his!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: KehI H!!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Don't hate me so! I promise you, I can help you! I'm your subordinate NOW!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Oh, so you can't get home?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm so sorry for what I did in the past! I've had a change of heart!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Alright, sure! If you've had a change of heart, then you're good now.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: WHATA-?! th- thank you!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: What the-?! is this a trap? Why'd he say yes so easily?! Now I'm scared for real!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: MM. LuffyTaro, this man uses quite interesting magic.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: He could be of use to us!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Remember someone

dialogue.speaker: Hyogoro
dialogue.text: Hm? HYogoro?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I don't believe it... I thought I was going to die a dog's death here...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'd accepted it! And if things had happened just a moment later, I really would have died. In the dark.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The Red Sheaths are alive! Oden's son, MOMONOSUKE

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: is alive! The fires of rebellion... still blaze on!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: raize, straw hat... now that it's come to this, this old man

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Still has a thing or two he needs to see done! Please, allow me to join you too!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You couldn't ask for a better ally than him, Luffy!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This man was once the grand boss of all the Yakuza in Wano!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: WHATA?! So you really are super strong, huh, old man!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Old man?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: By the way...it seems you all are searching for allies in the flower capital at the moment.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But I believe the fact that you came here, to union, must have also been fate!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: This mine...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: Current location

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: Has four other places

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That are the same size as this one!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There are tens of thousands of prisoners here in UDon.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But not all of them were arrested for stealing or assault.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The crime of the vast majority of the prisoners here

dialogue.speaker: Orochi
dialogue.text: Was rebellion against Orochi!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: I see! We totally overlooked that!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The anger of the entire country festers here!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The Yakuza bosses that upheld this country from the shadows... they're here too!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I shall speak to them! They will gladly fight for the Kozuki name!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: One last thing. Raiza! That cell...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: I know.

dialogue.speaker: Kawamatsu
dialogue.text: I have already spoken with Kawamatsu.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: I will release him

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: First thing!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I see! So after we destroy this place,

dialogue.speaker: Kinemon
dialogue.text: Every- one'll become our Allies!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: Luffy! Do not speak so loudly!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And I do not expect it to be so easy...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: UDon, Waste- Lands

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm sleepy and hungry and sleepy and hungry.

dialogue.speaker: Big Mom
dialogue.text: How much longer till the Oshiruko, Kiku?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It seems this crocoshark is rather tired too, but...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: We've already reached upon!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Are you sure that is a good thing?!

dialogue.speaker: Big Mom
dialogue.text: For better or worse...O-Lin draws nearer!

summary: This section advances the Wano - Hiyori and Yasuie storyline with 59 dialogue beats. Hiyori and Yasuie bring the rebellion’s emotional history into the foreground.
